readme.text:
# 🚀 Mwaki Denis — Developer Portfolio✨😁 Welcome to the official repository of my developer portfolio! A modern, sleek, and fully responsive personal website built using **Vite + React + TypeScript**, styled with **Tailwind CSS** and enhanced with **shadcn/ui** components. This portfolio is more than just a webpage — it's a complete showcase of my journey, skills, projects, and professional story as a passionate software engineer. --- ## 🔥 Features - 🎨 Beautiful UI/UX with **Dark/Light Mode Toggle** <<<<<<< HEAD - 📱 Fully responsive across all devices (320px - 2xl) - 💬 Chat Section with several sections - 🎬 Animated GIFs - 📜 About Me & Career Journey Timeline - 🧠 Technical Skills Overview with Progress Bars - 🏆 Certifications Section (Improved grid layout) - ✍️ Integrated Blog Highlights & Blog Section - 💼 Project Portfolio with: - Live GitHub API integration - Rich Descriptions - Live Demo Links - GitHub Repository Links - Dynamic Tech Stack Tags with vibrant colors ======= - 📱 Fully responsive across all devices - A Chat Section With Several sections😁😁😁😁😁😁😁😁 - Gifs - 📜 About Me & Career Journey Timeline - 🧠 Technical Skills Overview with Progress Bars - 🏆 Certifications Section - ✍️ Integrated Blog Highlights & Blog Section - 💼 Project Portfolio with: - Rich Descriptions - Live Demo Link - GitHub Repository Link - Stack Tags >>>>>>> 650c615b9e811d9733401503e6e894e73740400c - 🎓 Education History Section - 🧭 Career Timeline - 💬 Testimonials Carousel - ⭐ Interactive Rating/Feedback Section - 📞 Contact Form with Validation - 📍 Embedded Google Maps Location (optional) - 📱 Floating WhatsApp Contact Icon - 🧩 Modular, Reusable Components - 🧪 SEO Meta Tags & Social Sharing Optimized - 📂 Organized Codebase & Folder Structure - 🌍 Social Media Integration - 📎 Resume Download Link - 📌 Sticky Navbar & Smooth Scrolling - 🔗 External Link Previews (with rich cards) - 🧠 Clean and maintainable TypeScript Codebase <<<<<<< HEAD ======= - Added Pricing Info >>>>>>> 650c615b9e811d9733401503e6e894e73740400c --- ## 🛠️ Tech Stack - [Vite](https://vitejs.dev/) — lightning-fast dev server & build tool - [React](https://reactjs.org/) + [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) — for robust component logic - [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/) — utility-first styling - [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/) — elegant and accessible UI components --- ## 📦 Prerequisites Make sure you have **Node.js** and **npm** installed. We recommend installing them using **[nvm](https://github.com/nvm-sh/nvm)**: ```bash # Install nvm (if not installed) curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.7/install.sh | bash # Install latest LTS version of Node.js nvm install --lts # Verify installations node -v npm -v ``` --- ## 🚀 Getting Started Follow these steps to run the project locally: ### Step 1: Clone the repository ```bash git clone https://github.com/mwakidenis/portfolio_website.git ``` ### Step 2: Navigate to the project folder ```bash <<<<<<< HEAD cd portfolio_website-main ======= cd mwakidenis-portfolio >>>>>>> 650c615b9e811d9733401503e6e894e73740400c ``` ### Step 3: Install dependencies ```bash npm install ``` <<<<<<< HEAD ### Step 4: Create environment variables Create a `.env` file in the root directory: ```env VITE_GITHUB_TOKEN=your_github_token_here VITE_GITHUB_USERNAME=your_github_username ``` ### Step 5: Run the development server ======= ### Step 4: Run the development server >>>>>>> 650c615b9e811d9733401503e6e894e73740400c ```bash npm run dev ``` The site will be available at [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173) --- <<<<<<< HEAD ## 🌐 Deployment to Vercel ### Option 1: Deploy via Vercel CLI ```bash # Install Vercel CLI npm i -g vercel # Login to Vercel vercel login # Deploy vercel ``` ### Option 2: Deploy via GitHub 1. Push your code to a GitHub repository 2. Go to [Vercel Dashboard](https://vercel.com/dashboard) 3. Click "Add New..." → "Project" 4. Import your GitHub repository 5. Configure the project: - Framework Preset: `Vite` - Build Command: `npm run build` - Output Directory: `dist` 6. Add environment variables in Vercel: - `VITE_GITHUB_TOKEN` (optional, for higher API rate limits) - `VITE_GITHUB_USERNAME` (your GitHub username) 7. Click "Deploy" ### Environment Variables | Variable | Description | Required | |----------|-------------|----------| | `VITE_GITHUB_TOKEN` | GitHub Personal Access Token for API rate limits | No | | `VITE_GITHUB_USERNAME` | Your GitHub username (default: mwakidenis) | No | To create a GitHub token: 1. Go to GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens 2. Generate new token (classic) 3. Select `public_repo` scope 4. Copy the token and add to Vercel environment variables --- ## 📁 Project Structure ``` src/ ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # Page sections │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ ├── chat/ # Chat functionality │ └── *.tsx # Reusable components ├── data/ # Static data (projects, certificates) ├── hooks/ # Custom React hooks │ └── useGithubProjects.ts ├── lib/ # Utility functions ├── pages/ # Page components ├── types/ # TypeScript type definitions └── App.tsx # Main application ``` --- ## 🎨 Color System - **Primary Theme**: Coal Black (#0B0B0F, #111114, #16161C) - **Accent Colors**: Electric Blue, Neon Purple, Emerald, Amber, Crimson, Cyan, Pink, Lime - **Dynamic Tags**: Tech stack badges auto-rotate through accent colors --- ======= >>>>>>> 650c615b9e811d9733401503e6e894e73740400c ## 🌐 Live Demo 👉 [Visit My Live Portfolio](https://mwakidenis.pages.dev/) --- ## 📬 Contact <<<<<<< HEAD Have feedback or opportunities? Let's talk! ======= Have feedback or opportunities? Let’s talk! >>>>>>> 650c615b9e811d9733401503e6e894e73740400c #### [📧 Email:](mwakidenice@gmail.com) #### [💬 WhatsApp:](https://wa.me/254798750585) #### [🌍 LinkedIn:](https://www.linkedin.com/in/denis-it-54199939a/) #### [🐙 GitHub:](https://github.com/mwakidenis) --- ## 📝 License This project is open source and available under the [MIT License](LICENSE). --- ### 💡 Inspiration This portfolio was crafted to reflect my passion for software development, clean design, and user-focused experiences. Hope it inspires you too! --- ## Project Admin ⚡

mwakidenis

Contributors

I extend my heartfelt gratitude for any invaluable contribution to this project! Your efforts play a pivotal role in elevating the repo to greater heights. --- <<<<<<< HEAD Last updated: 2026-04-03 11:11 UTC ======= Last updated: 2026-04-03 11:11 UTC >>>>>>> 650c615b9e811d9733401503e6e894e73740400c

readme.text: # CommitCat 🐈‍⬛ A developer desktop companion that grows with your coding activity. ![CommitCat on desktop](assets/example.png) CommitCat lives on your desktop, watches your development environment locally, and reacts to your work — commits, coding time, focus sessions, and more. > 🐱 A tiny coding partner that never judges you (even at 3 AM). --- ## ✨ Features ### Core - 🐾 Desktop pet with pixel-art sprites — walks, sits, sleeps, and celebrates - 👣 Menu bar / system tray companion (streak tooltip) - 🖱️ Draggable — place it anywhere on your screen - 🖱️ Click-through — transparent areas pass clicks to the desktop - 💬 Click to chat (speech bubble reactions with personality) - 💬 Random coding messages — cat reacts while you code - 🎨 Multi-color cat (orange / brown / white) - 🐾 Petting — right-click & swipe to pet the cat with tiered reactions - 💤 Sleeps when you're away - 🖥️ Fullscreen-aware — auto-hides when you go fullscreen ### Coding Activity - 🐙 Local Git commit & push tracking — reacts every time you commit - 💻 IDE detection — knows when you're coding (VS Code, JetBrains, Xcode, and more) - 🌙 Late-night coding awareness - 🌱 XP & level system — grows with your activity - 🔥 Streak system — 3/7/30-day milestones with bonus XP - ✨ Pixel-art level-up animation with burst particles ### Focus & Productivity - ⏱️ Pomodoro focus timer with break timer - 📊 Daily summary timeline (right-click → Today) — view commits, coding time, XP earned, and event history ### Integrations - 🐙 GitHub integration — PR open/merge XP, star notifications - 🤖 Claude AI chat — double-click the cat to chat with your AI companion (Anthropic API) - 🧩 VSCode Extension — install from the [VSCode Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=commitcat.commitcat) for seamless IDE detection - 🔔 macOS Notification Center — system notifications for key events - 🔄 Auto-update checker — notifies when a new version is available ### IDE Plugins - 📦 [VS Code Extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=commitcat.commitcat) — coding time, file saves, build tracking - 📦 JetBrains Plugin (IntelliJ, WebStorm, PyCharm, GoLand, CLion, RustRover) — coding time, file saves, build tracking ### Settings - ⚙️ Settings panel — manage watched repos, cat color, timer durations, API keys, and XP progress - 🐳 Docker activity awareness — container start/build detection with XP --- ## 🧠 How It Grows CommitCat gains XP from: | Activity | XP | |---|---| | Git commit | +10 XP | | Git push | +5 XP | | 1 hour of coding | +5 XP | | Late-night session | +15 XP | | Pomodoro complete | +20 XP | | GitHub PR opened | +20 XP | | GitHub PR merged | +30 XP | | Docker container start | +5 XP | | Docker build complete | +15 XP | | 3-day streak | +50 XP | | 7-day streak | +100 XP | | 30-day streak | +500 XP | Level up formula: **Level n → n+1 requires n × 100 XP** The more you build, the happier it becomes. --- ## 🔒 Privacy First CommitCat is designed for developers who care about privacy. - ❌ No code is collected - ❌ No keystrokes are recorded - ❌ No files are uploaded - ❌ No telemetry by default ✔️ All data is stored locally on your machine ✔️ External integrations (GitHub, AI) are opt-in only --- ## 🖥️ Platforms - ✅ macOS - ✅ Windows - ✅ Linux --- ## 📦 Installation Download from the [Releases page](https://github.com/eunseo9311/commit-cat/releases). **macOS** ``` CommitCat.dmg ``` > **macOS Gatekeeper 경고 시:** > 앱이 코드사인되지 않아 "손상되었기 때문에 열 수 없습니다" 경고가 나올 수 있습니다. > 터미널에서 아래 명령어를 실행하면 해결됩니다: > ``` > xattr -cr /Applications/Commit\ Cat.app > ``` **Windows** ``` CommitCat-Setup.exe ``` **Linux** ``` CommitCat.AppImage # Most distros CommitCat.deb # Debian/Ubuntu ``` > **Wayland (Hyprland, Sway 등) 사용 시 EGL 에러가 발생하면:** > ``` > WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 commit-cat > ``` > 또는 X11 백엔드를 사용하세요: > ``` > GDK_BACKEND=x11 commit-cat > ``` ### VSCode Extension Search **"CommitCat"** in the VSCode Extensions tab, or install directly from the [Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=commitcat.commitcat). ### Build from source ```bash git clone https://github.com/eunseo9311/commit-cat.git cd commit-cat npm install npm run tauri dev ``` **Requirements:** Node.js, Rust, Tauri CLI --- ## 🛠️ Built With - [Tauri](https://tauri.app/) — lightweight desktop framework - [React](https://react.dev/) — UI - [Rust](https://www.rust-lang.org/) — system integration, Git & IDE tracking - [Anthropic API](https://docs.anthropic.com/) — AI chat (optional) --- ## 🗺️ Roadmap **MVP ✅** - [x] Desktop pet rendering - [x] Activity & IDE detection - [x] Local Git integration - [x] XP & growth system - [x] Tray / menu bar UI - [x] Settings panel **v1.0 — Local Complete ✅** - [x] Pomodoro focus timer with break timer - [x] Daily coding summary & event timeline - [x] GitHub integration (PR tracking, star notifications) - [x] Claude AI chat companion - [x] macOS / Windows / Linux support - [x] Docker integration - [x] IDE plugins (VS Code Extension, JetBrains Plugin) - [x] Petting interaction & click-through **v1.1 — Architecture Refactor ✅** - [x] Cargo workspace + `commit-cat-core` crate extraction - [x] Platform trait abstraction (`#[cfg]` cleanup) - [x] Remove duplicated model files in `src-tauri` - [x] State machine integration (core as single source of truth) **v1.2 — Design & Items ✅** - [x] Item system foundation (hats, accessories, inventory UI) - [x] Grab sprite system (color-specific drag animations) - [x] AI provider/model selection (Claude + OpenAI GPT) - [x] Cat design renewal - [x] Item positioning & motion polish (per-state sprite alignment) - [x] Auto-equip items (birthday hat, commit streak crown, etc.) **v1.3 — IDE Plugins 🧩** - [x] JetBrains plugin marketplace preparation - [ ] JetBrains Extension marketplace release **v1.4 — VSCode Webview 🖥️** - [ ] Cat rendering inside VSCode (Webview Panel) - [ ] Environment detection (desktop / Codespaces / web) **v2.0 — Cloud ☁️** - [ ] Cloud API server (Rust + Axum) - [ ] Cross-device sync (event-based, offline-first) - [ ] GitHub OAuth **v2.1 — Badge & Profile 🏅** - [x] GitHub README badge (`![CommitCat](api.commitcat.dev/badge/user)`) - [x] Public profile page - [ ] GitHub Codespaces support **v3.0 — Multi-Platform 🌐** - [ ] Discord bot integration - [ ] External app widgets - [ ] Community features (leaderboard, team stats) --- ## 🤝 Contributing Contributions are welcome! - Open an issue - Suggest features - Submit pull requests --- ## 📜 License - **Code:** AGPL-3.0 License - **Assets (sprites, icons, pixel art):** All rights reserved. See [public/assets/LICENSE](public/assets/LICENSE). Not for redistribution or reuse without permission. --- ## 💬 Status v2.1 in progress — badge & profile live at [commitcat-api.fly.dev](https://commitcat-api.fly.dev) If you like the idea, consider giving the repo a ⭐

items.name: CommitCat v0.3.1
items.body_excerpt:
## CommitCat v0.3.1

### Download

| 플랫폼 | 파일 | 설명 |
|--------|------|------|
| 🍎 macOS (Apple Silicon) | `.dmg` | Mac 설치 파일 (M1/M2/M3/M4) |
| 🪟 Windows | `.exe` (setup) | Windows 설치 파일 (권장) |
| 🪟 Windows | `.msi` | Windows MSI 설치 파일 |
| 🐧 Linux | `.AppImage` | Linux 실행 파일 (대부분의 배포판) |
| 🐧 Linux | `.deb` | Debian/Ubuntu 패키지 |

> ⚠️ 현재 Intel Mac은 지원하지 않습니다.

> **🍎 macOS 사용자 필독:** 앱이 아직 코드사인되지 않아, 처음 실행 시 **"손상되었기 때문에 열 수 없습니다"** 경고가 나올 수 있습니다.
> 터미널에서 아래 명령어를 실행하면 해결됩니다:
> ```
> xattr -cr /Applications/Commit\ Cat.app
> ```

### What's new
See [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/eunseo9311/commit-cat/commits/main) for details.

evidence.excerpt: ## CommitCat v0.3.1 ### Download | 플랫폼 | 파일 | 설명 | |--------|------|------| | 🍎 macOS (Apple Silicon) | `.dmg` | Mac 설치 파일 (M1/M2/M3/M4) | | 🪟 Windows | `.exe` (setup) | Windows 설치 파일 (권장) | | 🪟 Windows | `.msi` | Windows MSI 설치 파일 | | 🐧 Linux | `.AppImage` | Linux 실행 파일 (대부분의 배포판) | | 🐧 Linux | `.deb` | Debian/Ubuntu 패키지 | > ⚠️ 현재 Intel Mac은 지원하지 않습니다. > **🍎 macOS 사용자 필독:** 앱이 아직 코드사인되지 않아, 처음 실행 시 **"손상되었기 때문에 열 수 없습니다"** 경고가 나올 수 있습니다. > 터미널에서 아래 명령어를 실행하면 해결됩니다: > ``` > xattr -cr /Applications/Commit\ Cat.app > ``` ### What's new See [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/eunseo9311/commit-cat/commits/main) for details.

repo_cards.description: Memory engine and app that is extremely fast, scalable. The Memory API for the AI era.

readme.text:
State-of-the-art memory and context engine for AI.

Docs · Quickstart · Dashboard · Discord

--- Supermemory is the memory and context layer for AI. **#1 on [LongMemEval](https://github.com/xiaowu0162/LongMemEval), [LoCoMo](https://github.com/snap-research/locomo), and [ConvoMem](https://github.com/Salesforce/ConvoMem)** — the three major benchmarks for AI memory. We are a research lab building the engine, plugins and tools around it. Your AI forgets everything between conversations. Supermemory fixes that. It automatically learns from conversations, extracts facts, builds user profiles, handles knowledge updates and contradictions, forgets expired information, and delivers the right context at the right time. Full RAG, connectors, file processing — the entire context stack, one system. | | | |---|---| | 🧠 **Memory** | Extracts facts from conversations. Handles temporal changes, contradictions, and automatic forgetting. | | 👤 **User Profiles** | Auto-maintained user context — stable facts + recent activity. One call, ~50ms. | | 🔍 **Hybrid Search** | RAG + Memory in a single query. Knowledge base docs and personalized context together. | | 🔌 **Connectors** | Google Drive · Gmail · Notion · OneDrive · GitHub — auto-sync with real-time webhooks. | | 📄 **Multi-modal Extractors** | PDFs, images (OCR), videos (transcription), code (AST-aware chunking). Upload and it works. | All of this is in our single memory structure and ontology. --- ## Use Supermemory

🧑‍💻 I use AI tools

Build your own personal supermemory by using our app. Builds **persistent memory graph across every conversation**. Your AI remembers your preferences, projects, past discussions — and gets smarter over time. **[→ Jump to User setup](#give-your-ai-memory)**

🔧 I'm building AI products

Add memory, RAG, user profiles, and connectors to your agents and apps with **a single API**. No vector DB config. No embedding pipelines. No chunking strategies. **[→ Jump to developer quickstart](#build-with-supermemory-api)**
--- ## Give your AI memory The Supermemory App, browser extension, plugins and MCP server gives any compatible AI assistant persistent memory. One install, and your AI remembers you. ### The app You can use supermemory without any code, by using our consumer-facing app for free. Start at https://app.supermemory.ai It also comes with an agent embedded inside, which we call Nova. ### Supermemory Plugins Supermemory comes built with Plugins for Claude Code, OpenCode and OpenClaw. These plugins are implementations of the supermemory API, and they are open source! You can find them here: - Openclaw plugin: https://github.com/supermemoryai/openclaw-supermemory - Claude code plugin: https://github.com/supermemoryai/claude-supermemory - OpenCode plugin: https://github.com/supermemoryai/opencode-supermemory ### MCP - Quick install ```bash npx -y install-mcp@latest https://mcp.supermemory.ai/mcp --client claude --oauth=yes ``` Replace `claude` with your client: `cursor`, `windsurf`, `vscode`, etc. Read more about our MCP here - https://supermemory.ai/docs/supermemory-mcp/mcp ### What your AI gets | Tool | What it does | |---|---| | `memory` | Save or forget information. Your AI calls this automatically when you share something worth remembering. | | `recall` | Search memories by query. Returns relevant memories + your user profile summary. | | `context` | Injects your full profile (preferences, recent activity) into the conversation at start. In Cursor and Claude Code, just type `/context`. | ### How it works Once installed, Supermemory runs in the background: 1. **You talk to your AI normally.** Share preferences, mention projects, discuss problems. 2. **Supermemory extracts and stores the important stuff.** Facts, preferences, project context — not noise. 3. **Next conversation, your AI already knows you.** It recalls what you're working on, how you like things, what you discussed before. Memory is scoped with **projects** (container tags) so you can separate work and personal context, or organize by client, repo, or anything else. ### Supported clients **Claude Desktop** · **Cursor** · **Windsurf** · **VS Code** · **Claude Code** · **OpenCode** · **OpenClaw** The MCP server is open source — [view the source](https://supermemory.ai/docs/supermemory-mcp/mcp). ### Manual configuration Add this to your MCP client config: ```json { "mcpServers": { "supermemory": { "url": "https://mcp.supermemory.ai/mcp" } } } ``` Or use an API key instead of OAuth: ```json { "mcpServers": { "supermemory": { "url": "https://mcp.supermemory.ai/mcp", "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer sm_your_api_key_here" } } } } ``` --- ## Build with Supermemory (API) If you're building AI agents or apps, Supermemory gives you the entire context stack through one API — memory, RAG, user profiles, connectors, and file processing. ### Install ```bash npm install supermemory # or: pip install supermemory ``` ### Quickstart ```typescript import Supermemory from "supermemory"; const client = new Supermemory(); // Store a conversation await client.add({ content: "User loves TypeScript and prefers functional patterns", containerTag: "user_123", }); // Get user profile + relevant memories in one call const { profile, searchResults } = await client.profile({ containerTag: "user_123", q: "What programming style does the user prefer?", }); // profile.static → ["Loves TypeScript", "Prefers functional patterns"] // profile.dynamic → ["Working on API integration"] // searchResults → Relevant memories ranked by similarity ``` ```python from supermemory import Supermemory client = Supermemory() client.add( content="User loves TypeScript and prefers functional patterns", container_tag="user_123" ) result = client.profile(container_tag="user_123", q="programming style") print(result.profile.static) # Long-term facts print(result.profile.dynamic) # Recent context ``` Supermemory automatically extracts memories, builds user profiles, and returns relevant context. No embedding pipelines, no vector DB config, no chunking strategies. ### Framework integrations Drop-in wrappers for every major AI framework: ```typescript // Vercel AI SDK import { withSupermemory } from "@supermemory/tools/ai-sdk"; const model = withSupermemory(openai("gpt-4o"), "user_123"); // Mastra import { withSupermemory } from "@supermemory/tools/mastra"; const agent = new Agent(withSupermemory(config, "user-123", { mode: "full" })); ``` **Vercel AI SDK** · **LangChain** · **LangGraph** · **OpenAI Agents SDK** · **Mastra** · **Agno** · **Claude Memory Tool** · **n8n** ### Search modes ```typescript // Hybrid (default) — RAG + Memory in one query const results = await client.search.memories({ q: "how do I deploy?", containerTag: "user_123", searchMode: "hybrid", }); // Returns deployment docs (RAG) + user's deploy preferences (Memory) // Memories only const results = await client.search.memories({ q: "user preferences", containerTag: "user_123", searchMode: "memories", }); ``` ### User profiles Traditional memory relies on search — you need to know what to ask for. Supermemory automatically maintains a profile for every user: ```typescript const { profile } = await client.profile({ containerTag: "user_123" }); // profile.static → ["Senior engineer at Acme", "Prefers dark mode", "Uses Vim"] // profile.dynamic → ["Working on auth migration", "Debugging rate limits"] ``` One call. ~50ms. Inject into your system prompt and your agent instantly knows who it's talking to. ### Connectors Auto-sync external data into your knowledge base: **Google Drive** · **Gmail** · **Notion** · **OneDrive** · **GitHub** · **Web Crawler** Real-time webhooks. Documents automatically processed, chunked, and searchable. ### API at a glance | Method | Purpose | |---|---| | `client.add()` | Store content — text, conversations, URLs, HTML | | `client.profile()` | User profile + optional search in one call | | `client.search.memories()` | Hybrid search across memories and documents | | `client.search.documents()` | Document search with metadata filters | | `client.documents.uploadFile()` | Upload PDFs, images, videos, code | | `client.documents.list()` | List and filter documents | | `client.settings.update()` | Configure memory extraction and chunking | Full API reference → [supermemory.ai/docs](https://supermemory.ai/docs) --- ## Benchmarks Supermemory is state of the art across all major AI memory benchmarks: | Benchmark | What it measures | Result | |---|---|---| | **[LongMemEval](https://github.com/xiaowu0162/LongMemEval)** | Long-term memory across sessions with knowledge updates | **81.6% — #1** | | **[LoCoMo](https://github.com/snap-research/locomo)** | Fact recall across extended conversations (single-hop, multi-hop, temporal, adversarial) | **#1** | | **[ConvoMem](https://github.com/Salesforce/ConvoMem)** | Personalization and preference learning | **#1** | We also built **[MemoryBench](https://supermemory.ai/docs/memorybench/overview)** — an open-source framework for standardized, reproducible benchmarks of memory providers. Compare Supermemory, Mem0, Zep, and others head-to-head: ```bash bun run src/index.ts run -p supermemory -b longmemeval -j gpt-4o -r my-run ``` ### Benchmarking your own memory solution We provide an Agent skill for companies to benchmark their own context and memory solutions against supermemory. ``` npx skills add supermemoryai/memorybench ``` Simply run this and do `/benchmark-context` - Supermemory will automatically do the work for you! --- ## How memory works under the hood ``` Your app / AI tool ↓ Supermemory │ ├── Memory Engine Extracts facts, tracks updates, resolves contradictions, │ auto-forgets expired info ├── User Profiles Static facts + dynamic context built from engine, always fresh ├── Hybrid Search RAG + Memory in one query ├── Connectors Real-time sync from Google Drive, Gmail, Notion, GitHub... └── File Processing PDFs, images, videos, code → searchable chunks ``` **Memory is not RAG.** RAG retrieves document chunks — stateless, same results for everyone. Memory extracts and tracks *facts about users* over time. It understand

repo_cards.description: An end-to-end terminal testing framework for CLI and TUI experiences

readme.text:
# TUI Test

TUI Test is a framework for testing terminal applications. It provides a rich API for writing tests that interact with a terminal application across macOS, Linux, and Windows with a wide range of shells. It is built to be **fast**, **reliable**, and **easy to use**.

## Supported Runtimes

- Node.js 24.X, 22.X, 20.X, 18.X, 16.X (16.6.0 >=)
- Bun (1.3.5 >=)

## Installation

Install TUI Test as using `npm`:

```sh
npm i -D @microsoft/tui-test
```

or `yarn`

```sh
yarn add --dev @microsoft/tui-test
```

or `pnpm`

```sh
pnpm add -D @microsoft/tui-test
```

or `bun`

```sh
bun add -D @microsoft/tui-test
```

## Running Tests

Running tests is as simple as running TUI Test from the command line after installation:

```sh
npx @microsoft/tui-test
```

or with `bun`

```sh
bunx @microsoft/tui-test
```

## Capabilities

### Resilient • No flaky tests

**Auto-wait** TUI Test provides a rich API for interacting with the terminal. It waits for the terminal to be ready before executing commands, and it provides tooling for waiting for terminal renders before executing assertions.

**Tracing**. Configure test retry strategy, capture stdout & stderr, and create detailed terminal snapshots to eliminate flakes.

### Full isolation • Fast execution

**Terminal contexts**. TUI Test creates a new 'terminal context' for each test, which includes a new terminal and new underlying pty. This delivers full test isolation with zero overhead. Creating new terminal contexts only takes a handful of milliseconds.

### Multi-platform / Multi-shell • No more "it works in my shell"

**Multi-platform**. TUI Test supports testing on macOS, Linux, and Windows with a wide range of shells when installed: `cmd`, `windows powershell`, `powershell`, `bash`, `git-bash`, `fish`, `zsh`, and `xonsh`. 

> Note: Bun is only supported on macOS and Linux.

**Wide-support**. TUI Test uses [xterm.js](https://xtermjs.org/) to render the terminal, which is a widely used terminal emulator in projects like [VSCode](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode) and [Hyper](https://github.com/vercel/hyper).

## Examples

To find more TUI Test examples [check out the examples folder](./examples) or in TUI Test's [e2e tests](./test/).

### Terminal Program

This code snippet shows how to start the terminal with a specific program running.

```ts
import { test, expect } from "@microsoft/tui-test";

test.use({ program: { file: "git" } });

test("git shows usage message", async ({ terminal }) => {
  await expect(terminal.getByText("usage: git", { full: true })).toBeVisible();
});
```

### Terminal Screenshot

This code snippet shows how to take a screenshot of the terminal.

```ts
import { test, expect } from "@microsoft/tui-test";

test("take a screenshot", async ({ terminal }) => {
  terminal.write("foo")

  await expect(terminal.getByText("foo")).toBeVisible();
  await expect(terminal).toMatchSnapshot();
});
```

### Terminal Assertions

This code snippet shows how to use rich assertions of the terminal.

```ts
import { test, expect } from "@microsoft/tui-test";

test("make a regex assertion", async ({ terminal }) => {
  terminal.submit("ls -l")

  await expect(terminal.getByText(/total [0-9]{3}/g)).toBeVisible();
});
```

## Traces

TUI Test provides a rich tracing system to help you debug and diagnose issues with your tests. You can enable traces by setting the `trace` value in your `tui-test.config.ts` to `true` or by running the cli with the `-t/--trace` flag.

Traces are contain a replay of everything the terminal received and can be used to diagnose issues with your tests, especially when issues happen on different machines. Traces are stored in by default in the `tui-traces` folder in the root of your project and can be replayed via the `show-trace` command.

## Configuration

TUI Test can be configured via the `tui-test.config.[ts|js]` file in the root of your project. The following is an example of a configuration file:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from "@microsoft/tui-test";

export default defineConfig({
  retries: 3,
  trace: true
});

```

## Contributing

This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a
Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us
the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit https://cla.opensource.microsoft.com.

When you submit a pull request, a CLA bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide
a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (e.g., status check, comment). Simply follow the instructions
provided by the bot. You will only need to do this once across all repos using our CLA.

This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/).
For more information see the [Code of Conduct FAQ](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/) or
contact [opencode@microsoft.com](mailto:opencode@microsoft.com) with any additional questions or comments.

## Trademarks

This project may contain trademarks or logos for projects, products, or services. Authorized use of Microsoft
trademarks or logos is subject to and must follow
[Microsoft's Trademark & Brand Guidelines](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/legal/intellectualproperty/trademarks/usage/general).
Use of Microsoft trademarks or logos in modified versions of this project must not cause confusion or imply Microsoft sponsorship.
Any use of third-party trademarks or logos are subject to those third-party's policies.

items.body_excerpt:
## What's Changed
* feat: add lifecycle hooks by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/55
* build: bump to 0.0.4 by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/56


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/compare/0.0.3...0.0.4

## What's Changed
* fix: xterm headless by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/51


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/compare/0.0.2...0.0.3

## What's Changed
* fix: ignore sourcemaps in files to transform with swc by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/48
* fix: expect timeout loading from config by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/47
* fix: only use commitlint on main by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/49
* fix: expose exit result in Terminal class by @maxpaulus43 in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/46
* build: bump to 0.0.2 by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/50

## New Contributors
* @maxpaulus43 made their first contribution in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/46

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/compare/0.0.1...0.0.2

scout.summary: The repository hosts a modern, responsive developer portfolio built with Vite, React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS, featuring interactive elements like a dark/light mode toggle, animated GIFs, blog integration, and live GitHub project data. It shows active development but contains merge conflict markers and inconsistent setup instructions.
highlights: Built with a modern stack: Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
Fully responsive design supporting devices from 320px to 2xl screens
Includes dynamic features: GitHub API integration, dark/light mode, animated GIFs, and a timeline of career journey
Well-documented setup instructions using nvm, Node.js, and environment variables for GitHub API access
risks: README contains unresolved merge conflict markers (<<<<<<< HEAD / ======= / >>>>>>>), indicating potential instability or incomplete merges
Inconsistent directory name in setup instructions ("portfolio_website-main" vs "mwakidenis-portfolio") may confuse new contributors
Open issue for a Tetris game feature suggests scope creep or unfinished core functionality

inventor.summary: The repository demonstrates inventive integration of modern web technologies to create a dynamic, personalized developer portfolio with interactive features like live GitHub project data, animated content, and responsive UI components.
highlights: Innovative use of GitHub API integration to dynamically display live project data including descriptions, tech tags, and links
Creative combination of Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui for a performant and visually rich user experience
Inclusion of animated GIFs and a multi-section chat interface suggests experimental or playful UX enhancements beyond standard portfolios
Environment variable configuration (VITE_GITHUB_TOKEN, VITE_GITHUB_USERNAME) enables personalized, secure API access without hardcoding credentials
risks: Merge conflict markers in the README ([ID:E-RD-003], [ID:E-RD-007]) suggest recent collaborative edits were not cleanly resolved, risking broken setup instructions or inconsistent documentation
Open Tetris game feature request ([ID:E-IS-001]) indicates potential scope expansion into non-core functionality, which may distract from portfolio stability or purpose

engineer.summary: The repository implements a modern, responsive developer portfolio using Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui, with dynamic GitHub integration and interactive UI elements. However, it contains unresolved merge conflicts in the README and inconsistent setup instructions that could hinder onboarding and deployment.
highlights: Uses a robust, modern frontend stack (Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui) for performance and maintainability
Dynamically fetches project data via GitHub API using environment variables (VITE_GITHUB_TOKEN, VITE_GITHUB_USERNAME) for secure, personalized content
Fully responsive design supporting screen sizes from 320px to 2xl, with dark/light mode toggle and animated GIFs
Structured project organization evident from root files (vite.config.ts, tsconfig.*, tailwind.config.ts) indicating proper tooling setup
risks: Unresolved Git merge conflict markers in README ([ID:E-RD-003], [ID:E-RD-007]) may mislead developers during setup or indicate incomplete integration
Inconsistent directory names in setup commands ('portfolio_website-main' vs 'mwakidenis-portfolio') create ambiguity for new contributors
Open Tetris game feature request ([ID:E-IS-001]) suggests potential deviation from core portfolio functionality, risking scope creep

skeptic.summary: While the repository presents a polished developer portfolio with modern tech and dynamic features, it exhibits signs of instability, inconsistency, and potential overreach that undermine its reliability and focus.
highlights: Claims of 'fully responsive' design and 'live GitHub API integration' are plausible given the stated stack (Vite, React, Tailwind, shadcn/ui) and env config [E-RD-001, E-RD-004, E-RD-008]
Repository has modest popularity (50 stars, 1 fork) but minimal issue activity (only 2 issues), suggesting limited real-world validation or community engagement
risks: README contains unresolved Git merge conflict markers in both feature descriptions [E-RD-003] and setup commands [E-RD-007], indicating poor maintenance hygiene and risking user confusion or failed setups
Setup instructions reference two different directory names ('portfolio_website-main' vs 'mwakidenis-portfolio') [E-RD-007], contradicting each other and casting doubt on documentation accuracy
An open issue requests a Tetris game [E-IS-001], which is unrelated to a professional portfolio’s core purpose—raising concerns about scope discipline and project focus
Despite claims of 'improved grid layout' and 'blog highlights' [E-RD-003], there's no evidence of actual blog infrastructure or content in root files [E-RF-001], suggesting possible overstatement

scout.summary: CommitCat is a cross-platform desktop pet application built with Rust and Tauri that tracks developer activity (Git commits, IDE usage, focus sessions) and provides playful, interactive feedback through pixel-art animations, XP progression, and AI chat integration.
highlights: Interactive desktop pet with pixel-art sprites that responds to coding activity, commits, and focus sessions
Cross-platform support (macOS Apple Silicon, Windows, Linux) via Tauri with multiple distribution formats
Deep IDE integrations for VS Code and JetBrains products, plus Docker and GitHub awareness
Includes Pomodoro timer, streak tracking, XP/level system, and Claude AI chat via double-click
Active development with recent releases (v0.3.1) and clear feature roadmap
risks: No support for Intel-based Macs, limiting macOS compatibility
Unsigned macOS app triggers security warnings requiring manual terminal workaround
Reliance on external APIs (Anthropic/Claude) introduces dependency and potential cost/privacy concerns
Low issue activity (only 1 closed issue) may indicate limited community testing or feedback

inventor.summary: CommitCat is an inventive fusion of productivity tooling and playful interaction, embedding a reactive pixel-art desktop pet into the developer workflow through local activity monitoring, gamified progression, and AI-enhanced companionship.
highlights: Novel integration of a draggable, click-through desktop pet that responds in real time to Git commits, IDE usage, and focus sessions
Gamified developer experience with XP, levels, streaks, and animated rewards tied to coding behavior
Seamless cross-platform delivery via Tauri (Rust + web frontend) with native installers for macOS (Apple Silicon), Windows, and Linux
Embedded Claude AI chat triggered by double-click, blending emotional companionship with utility
Comprehensive local awareness including Docker builds, fullscreen detection, and late-night coding patterns
risks: Innovation relies on unsigned macOS binaries, creating friction for Apple Silicon users despite workarounds
Tight coupling with external APIs (e.g., Anthropic) may limit offline functionality or increase operational complexity
Lack of Intel Mac support excludes a segment of macOS developers, reducing platform inclusivity
Low community issue engagement suggests limited real-world validation of novel features

engineer.summary: CommitCat is a cross-platform desktop application built with Rust and Tauri that locally monitors developer activity (Git, IDEs, Docker) and provides interactive feedback via a pixel-art cat pet, gamified XP/streak systems, Pomodoro timers, and AI chat integration.
highlights: Built with Rust and Tauri (evidenced by Cargo.toml, src-tauri, and root structure), enabling native performance with web-based UI
Comprehensive local activity tracking: Git commits, VS Code/JetBrains/Xcode usage, Docker builds, and fullscreen detection
Rich feature set including draggable/click-through UI, multi-color sprites, sleep/idle behavior, and system tray integration
Gamification via XP, levels, streaks (3/7/30-day), and animated rewards tied to real coding behavior
AI integration through double-click Claude chat (Anthropic API) and extensible plugin architecture for IDEs
risks: macOS app lacks code signing, requiring manual xattr workaround on first launch (security and UX friction)
No support for Intel-based Macs limits macOS compatibility despite Apple Silicon focus
Dependency on external APIs (e.g., Anthropic) introduces potential cost, privacy, and reliability concerns
Low issue count (only 1 closed issue) suggests limited community testing or bug reporting

skeptic.summary: CommitCat presents an ambitious but potentially over-engineered desktop pet that promises deep developer integration, yet exhibits concerning gaps in platform support, security practices, and community validation.
highlights: Claims comprehensive local activity tracking (Git, IDEs, Docker) but offers no evidence of robust privacy safeguards or data handling policies
Promotes AI chat via Anthropic API without disclosing cost implications, usage limits, or offline behavior
Advertises cross-platform support while explicitly excluding Intel Macs—a significant macOS user segment
Relies on unsigned macOS binaries, forcing users to bypass system security with terminal commands
risks: Unsigned macOS app distribution violates standard security practices and may deter enterprise or cautious users
Absence of Intel Mac support contradicts claims of broad cross-platform compatibility
Heavy feature set (AI, gamification, IDE plugins) lacks corresponding community engagement—only 1 closed issue suggests minimal real-world testing
Dependency on external APIs (Claude, GitHub) introduces fragility; failure modes or rate limits could degrade core experience

scout.summary: Supermemory is a high-performance AI memory and context engine that automatically manages user profiles, handles dynamic knowledge updates, and integrates RAG with personalized memory. It supports multi-modal data ingestion and real-time connectors, positioning itself as a comprehensive context layer for AI applications.
highlights: Claims top rankings on three major AI memory benchmarks: LongMemEval, LoCoMo, and ConvoMem.
Offers hybrid search combining RAG and personalized memory in a single query.
Features automatic fact extraction, contradiction handling, and time-aware forgetting.
Provides prebuilt connectors for Google Drive, Gmail, Notion, OneDrive, and GitHub with real-time sync.
Supports multi-modal input including PDFs, images (OCR), videos (transcription), and code with AST-aware chunking.
Delivers user context in ~50ms via a dedicated API.
risks: Open issue reports a critical MCP OAuth failure with HTTP 500 errors, indicating potential authentication instability.
No GitHub releases despite significant stars (21k+) and claimed production readiness, suggesting possible immaturity or irregular versioning.
Heavy reliance on external services (e.g., Cloudflare, Postgres) may introduce operational complexity or vendor lock-in.

inventor.summary: Supermemory is an AI-native memory engine that invents a unified context layer by combining real-time user memory, RAG, and multi-modal data ingestion into a single low-latency system. It introduces novel mechanisms for handling temporal facts, contradictions, and automatic forgetting, while integrating deeply with productivity platforms via prebuilt connectors.
highlights: Invents a hybrid memory architecture that merges conversational memory with traditional RAG, enabling context-aware queries that blend personal history and knowledge bases.
Introduces automated memory lifecycle management—extracting facts, resolving contradictions, and expiring outdated information without manual intervention.
Pioneers AST-aware code chunking and multi-modal extraction (PDF, image OCR, video transcription) as native ingestion pathways for memory construction.
Achieves top rankings on three independent AI memory benchmarks (LongMemEval, LoCoMo, ConvoMem), indicating novel algorithmic or architectural advantages.
Delivers personalized user context in ~50ms through a purpose-built API, suggesting custom indexing or caching innovations.
risks: Core authentication component (MCP OAuth) is failing with 500 errors, threatening the reliability of identity-linked memory features.
Absence of versioned releases despite production claims raises concerns about stability and reproducibility of the invented systems.
Tight integration with Cloudflare stack (Workers, KV, Pages) may limit portability and increase dependency on a single cloud provider’s evolution.

engineer.summary: Supermemory is a TypeScript-based AI memory engine built with a modern stack (Remix, Cloudflare Workers, Drizzle ORM, Postgres) that delivers low-latency contextual memory by combining RAG, user profiling, and multi-modal ingestion. It emphasizes performance (~50ms context retrieval), real-time sync via connectors, and automated memory lifecycle management.
highlights: Built with a full-stack TypeScript architecture including Remix, Cloudflare Workers, and Drizzle ORM, targeting high scalability and low latency.
Implements a unified context API that merges RAG and personalized memory, enabling hybrid queries in ~50ms.
Supports real-time data ingestion from Google Drive, Gmail, Notion, OneDrive, and GitHub via webhooks.
Features advanced multi-modal processing: OCR for images, transcription for videos, and AST-aware chunking for code.
Automates memory operations—fact extraction, contradiction resolution, and time-based forgetting—without manual intervention.
Validated on three major AI memory benchmarks (LongMemEval, LoCoMo, ConvoMem), indicating robust underlying engineering.
risks: Critical MCP OAuth integration is failing with HTTP 500 errors (open issue), which could break authentication-dependent memory features.
No versioned releases despite 21k+ stars and production claims, raising concerns about deployment stability and dependency management.
Deep coupling with Cloudflare ecosystem (Workers, KV, Pages) may complicate on-prem or multi-cloud deployments.

skeptic.summary: Supermemory makes bold claims about benchmark dominance and system capabilities, but lacks verifiable proof of stability, versioning, or reproducibility—raising doubts about its production readiness despite high popularity.
highlights: Claims #1 rankings on three AI memory benchmarks (LongMemEval, LoCoMo, ConvoMem) without linking to verifiable results or methodology.
Advertises a full-featured, low-latency memory API (~50ms) and real-time connectors, yet provides no performance benchmarks or SLA documentation.
Showcases multi-modal ingestion and hybrid RAG+memory as core innovations, but offers no architectural details or open evaluation datasets.
risks: Critical MCP OAuth failure (HTTP 500) remains unresolved in an open issue, undermining trust in authentication and user-linked memory integrity.
Zero GitHub releases despite 21k+ stars and claimed production use suggest the project may be pre-release or inconsistently maintained.
Heavy marketing language in README ('state-of-the-art', 'entire context stack') contrasts with absence of technical depth or independent validation.

scout.summary: TUI Test is a Microsoft-backed, cross-platform terminal testing framework built for reliability and speed, supporting a wide range of shells and leveraging xterm.js for terminal emulation. It emphasizes test isolation, auto-waiting, tracing, and ease of use, with active recent development but very early versioning (v0.0.4).
highlights: Built by Microsoft and integrates with widely adopted tools like xterm.js (used in VS Code).
Supports comprehensive shell coverage across macOS, Linux, and Windows (e.g., bash, zsh, PowerShell, cmd).
Features strong test isolation via per-test terminal contexts with minimal overhead.
Includes advanced capabilities like auto-waiting for terminal readiness and detailed tracing/snapshots to reduce flakiness.
Recent releases show active maintenance with fixes and new features like lifecycle hooks.
risks: Extremely early version (0.0.4) suggests immaturity and potential instability for production use.
No open issues may indicate low community adoption or lack of issue reporting rather than software completeness.
Limited release history (only 3 releases) implies the project is still in initial development phases.

inventor.summary: TUI Test is an innovative terminal testing framework that introduces a novel combination of auto-waiting, per-test terminal contexts, and xterm.js-based rendering to enable fast, reliable, and isolated end-to-end tests for CLI and TUI applications across major platforms and shells.
highlights: Introduces 'terminal contexts' that provide full test isolation with near-zero overhead by spinning up dedicated terminals and PTYs per test (milliseconds latency).
Implements auto-wait logic that synchronizes test execution with terminal readiness and render cycles, reducing flakiness without manual delays.
Leverages xterm.js—the same terminal emulator used in VS Code—for consistent, real-world terminal emulation during testing.
Supports an unusually broad set of shells (cmd, PowerShell, bash, zsh, fish, xonsh, etc.) across Windows, macOS, and Linux from a single API.
Enables detailed tracing with stdout/stderr capture, retry strategies, and terminal snapshots to debug and stabilize tests.
risks: Core innovation relies on early-stage infrastructure (v0.0.4), with recent fixes indicating unresolved stability issues (e.g., xterm headless mode).
Novel features like auto-wait and terminal contexts lack long-term validation due to minimal release history and absence of community-reported issues.
Heavy dependency on xterm.js may limit fidelity for edge cases not covered by its headless rendering implementation.

engineer.summary: TUI Test is a cross-platform terminal testing framework built in TypeScript that enables fast, isolated, and reliable end-to-end tests for CLI/TUI applications using xterm.js-based emulation and per-test terminal contexts. It supports major shells across Windows, macOS, and Linux, with features like auto-waiting, tracing, and lifecycle hooks, though it remains in a very early (v0.0.4) and potentially unstable state.
highlights: Uses per-test terminal contexts with dedicated PTYs for strong isolation and sub-10ms setup overhead [ID:E-RD-005].
Auto-wait mechanism synchronizes test steps with terminal readiness and render cycles to reduce flakiness [ID:E-RD-003].
Built on xterm.js—the same emulator used in VS Code—ensuring realistic terminal behavior [ID:E-RD-007].
Supports an extensive list of shells (bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell, cmd, etc.) across all major OSes [ID:E-RD-006].
Recent v0.0.4 release added lifecycle hooks, indicating active feature development [ID:E-RL-001].
risks: Extremely early version (0.0.4) with recent fixes for core functionality (e.g., xterm headless mode) suggests instability [ID:E-RL-002].
Lack of open issues may reflect low adoption rather than robustness, limiting real-world validation [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
Heavy reliance on xterm.js in headless mode may not fully replicate native terminal edge cases [ID:E-RD-007, E-RL-002].

skeptic.summary: TUI Test presents ambitious claims about speed, reliability, and cross-platform terminal testing, but its extremely early version (0.0.4), minimal release history, and lack of community engagement raise serious doubts about its maturity, stability, and real-world readiness.
highlights: Claims of 'fast', 'reliable', and 'zero overhead' test isolation [ID:E-RD-001, E-RD-005] are unsupported by performance benchmarks or third-party validation.
Despite supporting many shells and platforms [ID:E-RD-006], there is no evidence of comprehensive compatibility testing or edge-case handling beyond basic examples.
Reliance on xterm.js for terminal emulation [ID:E-RD-007] may not accurately replicate native terminal behavior, especially given recent fixes to headless mode [ID:E-RL-002].
risks: The project is pre-1.0 (v0.0.4) with only three patch releases, all authored primarily by a single contributor, indicating high risk of instability and incomplete feature coverage [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
Zero open issues and low star count (149) suggest limited adoption, making it difficult to assess real-world reliability or maintainability [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
Critical features like auto-wait and terminal contexts [ID:E-RD-003, E-RD-005] lack documentation on failure modes or timeout behaviors, increasing risk of hidden flakiness.
